Figure 3: Results under varying model shift levels
Figure 4: Results under varying precision shift levels

Figure 7: Comparison of source data selection results. (a) Trans-GLM performed state-level binary selection. (b) TransL2E employed sample-level selection. The sample selection proportions of each source state for TransL2E are: AL: 0.45, AR: 0.41, GA: 0.47, LA: 0.48, TX: 0.50, MS: 0.41, SC: 0.39, and NC: 0.40.
Figure 5: Results under varying feature dimensions
Figure 2: Results under varying outlier proportions in the source datasets.

Figure 1: Boxplots of source selection proportions (left) and corresponding estimation errors (right) with varying model shift level σ and 10% outlier contamination for different constructions of pj over 50 replicates. Outlier are present only in the source data. Simulations follow the setup of Experiment 2 in Section 4.2 with a single source dataset of size n = 1200. The black dashed line (right panel) indicates the average estimation error (0.38) of L2E fitted using only the target data.
